primary_legislation.england_wales: Environmental Protection Act 1990; Hazardous Waste Regulations 2005 (England and Wales); Controlled Waste (England and Wales) Regulations 2012
primary_legislation.scotland: Environmental Protection Act 1990; Special Waste Regulations 1996 (Scotland); SEPA guidance
primary_legislation.northern_ireland: Waste and Contaminated Land (Northern Ireland) Order 1997; Hazardous Waste Regulations (NI) 2005

notes: Key difference from school: GP practices generate clinical waste streams that are more extensive than schools. A detailed clinical waste logbook is maintained separately in GP-MOD-025. This module covers non-clinical hazardous waste (fluorescent tubes, batteries, IT equipment, printer cartridges, oil/fuel storage) and general waste duty of care. Clinical waste is intentionally cross-referenced rather than duplicated here.

logbooks.title: Hazardous Waste Disposal Record (Non-Clinical)
logbooks.frequency_detail: Record each disposal event as it occurs. Note: clinical and medicines waste is recorded in GP-MOD-025.

steps.description: Identify the waste type and confirm whether it is classified as hazardous. Non-clinical hazardous waste common to GP practices: fluorescent tubes and compact fluorescent lamps, batteries (all chemistries), waste electrical and electronic equipment (WEEE — computers, monitors, printers, medical devices at end of life), printer toner cartridges, aerosol cans.

Arrange collection with a licensed waste carrier — confirm the carrier holds an EA waste carrier licence (England/Wales), SEPA registration (Scotland), or NIEA registration (NI). Confirm they can handle the specific waste type.

Complete a Hazardous Waste Consignment Note for hazardous waste movements. Retain copies for minimum 3 years. Report each consignment to EA if the premises is a 'notifiable' hazardous waste producer (>500 kg per year). Check current EA guidance on premises exemptions.
Complete a Hazardous Waste Consignment Note. Retain copies for minimum 3 years. NRW regulates in Wales — confirm NRW carrier registration.
In Scotland, use a Special Waste Consignment Note. Pre-notification to SEPA is required at least 1 working day in advance for 'special waste' movements. Retain copies for minimum 3 years.
Complete a Hazardous Waste Consignment Note per NIEA requirements. NIEA-registered carrier required. Retain copies for minimum 3 years.
Complete a Waste Transfer Note for non-hazardous waste collections. Retain for minimum 2 years.
Record: date, waste type, EWC/LoW code, quantity, carrier name and licence number, consignment/transfer note reference number.

logbooks.title: Annual Oil/Fuel Storage Tank Inspection
logbooks.applicable_condition: Only required for practices with oil-fired heating systems or on-site fuel storage. Many modern GP premises use mains gas — check with the premises manager.

steps.description: Visually inspect the oil tank external surfaces for corrosion, leaks, damage or discolouration.
steps.photo_guidance: Photograph the tank, bund and any visible corrosion or staining.

steps.description: Inspect the secondary containment (bund): structurally sound, no cracks, free of standing water and oil. Confirm it can hold 110% of the largest tank volume.
Check fill point, vent pipe, OPD (overfill prevention device), fire valve, and oil level gauge — all operational.
Compliance with Control of Pollution (Oil Storage) Regulations 2001 (England) required for tanks >200 litres. EA enforces.
Environmental Permitting (England and Wales) Regulations 2016 apply to oil storage in Wales. NRW enforces.
Water Environment (Oil Storage) (Scotland) Regulations 2006 apply. SEPA enforces. Requirements broadly equivalent to England.
Control of Pollution (Oil Storage) Regulations (NI) 2010 apply. NIEA enforces.
Record: date, tank reference, condition of tank/bund/OPD/valve, any defects, action taken, inspector name.
